pr.title: items: match 19 functions across item files
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: Decompiled 19 previously stubbed item functions across many item-specific files, with associated header/prototype cleanup and item-var struct refinements. The PR reports all functions as 100% fuzzy-match and a clean ninja build. Most changes are small item behavior callbacks: animation/physics transitions, destroyed/picked-up handlers, velocity/timer updates, effect cleanup, and fighter callback integration for Pokemon/projectile-like items.
what_changed: Replaced placeholder comments with matched implementations for 19 item functions listed in the PR body: it_802C8C34, it_2725_Logic80_PickedUp, it_802AFEA8, it_802C573C, itFlipper_UnkMotion5_Anim, itFlipper_UnkMotion6_Anim, it_802E31F8, it_2725_Logic39_Destroyed, it_2725_Logic90_Destroyed, itPatapata_UnkMotion7_Anim, it_802D2A58, it_802B3F20, it_802BFE5C, it_802CB844, it_802CA8DC, it_802E2E30, itTincle_UnkMotion3_Phys, it_802CA014, and itLipstickspore_UnkMotion0_Phys.
Added or corrected concrete function prototypes in item headers, replacing several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with typed Item_GObj, Fighter_GObj, and bool/void signatures.

Extended itCommonItems.h with itLipstickSpore_ItemVars containing xDD4 and xDD8 fields, added itTincle_ItemVars.x4C by reducing padding, changed itPokemon_ItemVars.xE44 into a float/s32 union, and exposed itOldottosea_ItemVars.x24 as an s32 instead of padding.
Implemented item state transitions through Item_80268E5C and ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE in several callbacks, including Clink milk pickup, Oldottosea, Whitebea, Flipper, Ness yoyo, and Houou behavior.

Implemented fighter/item linkage cleanup callbacks: climbers ice destroyed calls Popo or Kirby init helpers based on item kind and then nulls the stored pointer; Pikachu thunder destroyed conditionally calls ftPk_SpecialLw_SetState_Unk0.

Implemented effect cleanup and hitlag callback setup where needed: Mewtwo shadowball destroys all effects and clears an item var before Item_8026A8EC; Houou assigns efLib_PauseAll, efLib_ResumeAll, an accessory callback, and a flag.

Implemented small physics/timer routines: Lipstick spore decelerates horizontal velocity toward zero by xDD8; Tincle applies vertical acceleration until a counter expires; Kabigon decrements a Pokemon item timer stored as s32 and respawns/resets when it reaches zero.

Implemented position/velocity helper routines: Pikachu tjoltair reads bone 6 position with lb_8000B1CC when a dynamic field is present; Ness yoyo stores a velocity vector, normalizes x sign against facing_dir, and changes animation; Matadogas scales all velocity axes according to item kind attributes.
Implemented dispatch wrappers such as it_802AFEA8 for thrown Seak needle, guarding null gobj/owner_gobj and dispatching arg2 values 0 and 1 to it_802AFF08 or it_802B00F4.
smart_moves: Used precise item-var layout changes instead of broad casts where the diff had evidence for named fields, e.g. adding itLipstickSpore_ItemVars.xDD8 for deceleration and exposing Tincle x4C and Oldottosea x24.
Converted Pokemon xE44 to a union of f32 and s32 so code needing integer countdown semantics could match without losing evidence that the same storage may be interpreted as float elsewhere.
Improved function prototypes alongside implementations, reducing reliance on UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and making later matching work easier across item files.
Preserved item-engine idioms already present in the codebase: GET_ITEM/gobj->user_data access, Item_80268E5C state changes, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE, Item_8026A8EC cleanup, and xDD4_itemVar per-item storage.

Added targeted includes for real dependencies rather than leaving placeholder declarations: fighter special headers for Pikachu/Kirby/Popo interactions, efLib for effect callbacks/cleanup, lb_00B0 for bone-position lookup, and itCommonItems.h where item attributes/vars were needed.
Kept many functions small and local to the relevant item file, which is appropriate for decomping isolated item callbacks and minimizes unrelated churn.
decomp_lessons: When matching item callbacks, first recover the relevant xDD4_itemVar sub-struct fields; exposing a real field or union in itCommonItems.h can be the key to clean C and correct codegen.
Timer fields may need signed-integer access even when nearby or previously known fields are floats; a union can document uncertain/shared storage while matching decrement-and-compare code.
Replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS in headers should be done as soon as callers and implementations reveal exact signatures; this propagates type information and can unlock further matches.

Item destroyed and picked-up callbacks often pair item-state cleanup with fighter callbacks or effect cleanup, so matching them may require importing fighter/effect headers rather than treating the function as item-only.
For simple velocity damping, matching code may require explicit sign branches and manual absolute-value logic rather than helper calls.
Item state transitions commonly use numeric motion IDs with ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE or literal flags such as 0x12; evidence should come from the matched code rather than naming the motion prematurely.
Dynamic bone lookups through xBBC_dynamicBoneTable and lb_8000B1CC are a useful pattern for projectile/item position helpers tied to fighter bones.
naming_conventions: Preserved existing address-style unknown names such as it_802CA014, it_802CB844, and it_802E31F8 when semantic names were not established.
Used item-specific callback naming already present in the project, e.g. itFlipper_UnkMotion5_Anim, itPatapata_UnkMotion7_Anim, itTincle_UnkMotion3_Phys, and itLipstickspore_UnkMotion0_Phys.
Logic callbacks follow the existing item convention it_2725_LogicXX_Destroyed or it_2725_LogicXX_PickedUp.
Item variable structs use the pattern it<ItemName>_ItemVars with offset-like member names such as xDD4, xDD8, x4C, and xE44_s32 when semantics are still unknown.
Header prototypes keep address comments and replace only the type information when known, maintaining navigability for decomp work.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8) in functions such as itPatapata_UnkMotion7_Anim and it_802B3F20, indicating stack-shape padding was needed for matching.
Used M2C_FIELD(ip, f32*, 0xE3C) in it_802CA8DC where a field was still not structurally modeled but exact offset access was needed for matching.
Used explicit null checks and early returns in wrappers like it_802AFEA8 and it_802C8C34 to match control flow.
Used explicit cast patterns such as GET_ITEM((HSD_GObj*) gobj), gobj->user_data, and HSD_GObjEvent casts where required by surrounding APIs and matching constraints.
Matched countdown logic by pre-decrementing xE44_s32 into a local timer before comparing against zero in it_802CA014.
Matched float sign handling manually, including explicit zero comparisons, greater-than/less-than branches, and no library fabs call.
review_feedback: No review comments or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body states verification by clean ninja build and fuzzy_match_percent: 100.0 for all functions, but no reviewer discussion is available to confirm additional rationale.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename address-style item functions from this PR to semantic names.
Check subsequent struct-layout PRs touching itPokemon_ItemVars.xE44, itTincle_ItemVars.x4C, itOldottosea_ItemVars.x24, or itLipstickSpore_ItemVars for confirmed semantics.
Search for follow-up fixes to itFlipper_UnkMotion6_Anim because the diff excerpt shows a bool function body calling it_80291344 without an explicit return.
Search for follow-up fixes to it_802AFEA8 because the diff excerpt shows an Item_GObj* return type with bare returns and no returned value in handled cases.
Compare other Pokemon item destroyed callbacks for the same fighter callback cleanup pattern used by climbers ice and Pikachu thunder.
